zh.lifeSummary: 當richard le childe於大約1282年出生在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾時，他的父親john le childe27歲，母親Alice Atte Wode26歲。他和Alice Wode至少有3個兒子。他去世於1348年在英國武斯特斯爾諾維其可享年67歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾布洛可利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当richard le childe于大约1282年出生在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾时，他的父亲john le childe27岁，母亲Alice Atte Wode26岁。他和Alice Wode至少有3个儿子。他去世于1348年在英国武斯特斯爾諾維其可享年67岁，埋葬在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾布洛可利。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: childe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Child
meanings: nickname from Middle English child ‘child, infant’ (Old English cild), in various possible applications. The word is found in Old English as a byname, and in Middle English as a widely used affectionate term of address. It was also used as a term of status for a young man of noble birth, although the exact meaning is not clear; in the 13th and 14th centuries it was a technical term used of a young noble awaiting elevation to the knighthood. In other cases it may have been applied as a byname to a youth considerably younger than his brothers or to one who was a minor on the death of his father.
in Kent, possibly a topographic name from Old English cielde ‘spring (water)’, a rare word derived from c(e)ald ‘cold’.

nameMeanings.name: richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When richard le childe was born about 1282, in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, john le childe, was 27 and his mother, Alice Atte Wode, was 26. He had at least 3 sons with Alice Wode. He died in 1348, in Northwick, Worcestershire, England, at the age of 67, and was buried in Blockley, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque richard le childe est né environ 1282, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, john le childe, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Alice Atte Wode, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils avec Alice Wode. Il est décédé en 1348, à Northwick, Worcestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Blockley, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als richard le childe um 1282 in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater john le childe 27 und seine Mutter Alice Atte Wode 26. Er hatte mit Alice Wode mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb 1348 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Northwick, Worcestershire, England und wurde in Blockley, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando richard le childe è nato il circa 1282 nel Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, john le childe, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Alice Atte Wode, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli con Alice Wode. Morì nel 1348, nel Northwick, Worcestershire, England, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolto a Blockley, Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: richard le childeが約1282年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアで生まれた時，父john le childeは27歳，母Alice Atte Wodeは26歳であった。彼はAlice Wodeとの間に少なくとも3人の息子をもうけた。彼は1348年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアノースウィックで67歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアブロックリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: richard le childe(이)가 대략 1282년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 john le childe(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Atte Wode(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Alice Wode(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1348년 영국 워스스터실르 노르스윅에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 블록리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando richard le childe nasceu aproximadamente 1282, em Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, john le childe, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Alice Atte Wode, tinha 26 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos com Alice Wode. Ele faleceu em 1348, em Northwick, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, com 67 anos, e foi sepultado em Blockley, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда richard le childe родился около 1282 г. в местности ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени john le childe было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Atte Wode, 26. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына от Alice Wode. Он умер в 1348 г. в местности нортвикк, ворсестиршир, Англия в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности блоккли, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando richard le childe nació aproximadamente 1282, en Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, john le childe, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Alice Atte Wode, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos con Alice Wode. Murió en 1348, en Northwick, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultado en Blockley, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
